text: This is the bronchoscopic image showing the mass in the right main bronchus including its lumen.
Bronchoscopic guided debulking was done.
Seeing the patency of the right upper lobe bronchus and bronchus intermedius on the bronchoscopy, it was decided to go ahead with a topotic total lung sparing surgery using four ports.
Lung is retracted anteriorly using a prograsp forceps and mediastinal pleura opened.
Right main bronchus, right upper lobe bronchus and bronchus intermedius were dissected meticulously and looped.
Here we can see the looping of right upper lobe bronchus
Then looping of bronchus intermedius.
Right upper lobe bronchus is cut open and then divided distal to the tumor leaving some healthy margins using robotic scissors.
Robotic scissors can be coupled with energy devices which helps in sharp dissection and also hemostasis of small bleeders on the bronchial wall simultaneously thus reducing the operative time.
Right main bronchus is similarly cut open leaving adequate margins beyond the tumour and then transected completely under vision.
Finally, bronchus intermedius was divided distal to the tumour with adequate margins.
Secondary coronal reconstruction was started by suturing adjacent walls of right upper lower bronchus and bronchus intermedius.
Here we used a trio V-lock suture.
V-lock suture is a dual angle barbed absorbable suture made up of glycolide, dioxanone and trimethylene carbonate.
We can see here the needle is being passed through a preformed anchor loop thus avoiding knots.
This is especially useful in vats and robotic surgeries where tying knots becomes a tedious process.
We can see the secondary carina reconstruction is being completed.
Now, right main bronchus is sutured to the reconstructed secondary carina.
First bite is taken at the membranocartilaginous junction more towards the cartilaginous side.
Again, the needle is passed through the preformed loop.

Here, at this point, suturing between the right main bronchus and reconstructed secondary carina is an important step wherein we need to take a tri-point stitch so as to get right main bronchus, bronchus intermedius and right upper lobe bronchus together, avoiding any gap between the three.
Suturing is done on both sides consecutively to achieve uniform tension on the suture line.
Now, membranous wall is started.
Here, posterior wall of the right upper lobe bronchus is sutured to the membranous wall of the right main bronchus.
For total reconstruction, we require a total of three sutures.
One for secondary carinal reconstruction and one each on both the sides for suturing right main bronchus and secondary carina.

Suturing is completed by taking a tri-point stitch between the right upper lobe, right main bronchus and bronchus intermedius similar to the one which has been taken previously and the sutures are tied.
Saline test was done to check for any major air leak and none was found.
And this is a 6 months follow up bronchoscopy showing patent right Apollo bronchus and bronchus intermedius.
Thank you.
